article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: Before putting into service or using a high-risk AI system at the workplace, deployers who are employers shall inform workers' representatives and the affected workers that they will be subject to the use of the high-risk AI system.
scoring_text: Knowledge of workplace AI deployment notification requirements, including worker representative consultation procedures, affected worker notification, and employment law compliance for AI system deployment.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of workplace AI deployment notification requirements, including worker representative consultation procedures, affected worker notification, and employment law compliance for AI system deployment.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 26(7) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.587, cosine: 0.640). Secondary: AG-K-006 (AG, cosine: 0.628), DG-T-007 (DG, cosine: 0.625).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.587). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [affected, consultation, deployment, employment, law, notification] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, categories, conformity, documentation, eu] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.913 (P=0.909, R=0.916). Balanced P/R — the 23-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.640 > STS 0.587 (gap +0.053). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 25.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [affected, consultation, deployment, employment, law, notification]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, categories, conformity, documentation, eu].
